holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for INTC right now — just triple witching ~80 days out, which is a tape event, not a fundamental one. The position is green on the day (+3.1%) but giving back over the week (-6.6%), so price is chopping without a near-term print to resolve it. Until an earnings date or foundry/product update lands on the calendar, there's no defined window to lean into.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro tape event, not company-specific)
INTC.bull: Turnaround optionality intact; today's bounce suggests buyers are still defending the recent pullback.
INTC.bear: -6.6% on the week with no dated catalyst to force a re-rate means weakness can drift further on tape flows.

NVDA.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — the next earnings print isn't in the feed, and the only thing ahead is triple witching ~80 days out, which is a tape event, not a fundamental one. The -6.55% over five days with a +1.54% bounce today suggests the name is digesting a pullback rather than breaking; without a confirmed catalyst to anchor an add or trim window, this is a sit-on-hands setup until an earnings date prints or price action forces the issue.
NVDA.bull: Pullback in an AI leader with a 1d bounce; reclaiming the 5d high would re-open the trend.
NVDA.bear: No dated catalyst to defend the name; if the -6.55% extends, there's nothing scheduled to break the drawdown.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down ~16% over five sessions with no dated company catalyst on the board — that's a hard tape to fade or chase without an anchor. The next real catalyst is the fiscal Q1 print (typically mid-September), which lines up uncomfortably close to Sep 18 triple witching; until a confirmed earnings date prints, this is a price-action read, not a catalyst read. Wait for either a confirmed earnings date or a stabilization/reclaim before committing to add — knife-catching a -16% week with no event in hand is the worst risk/reward.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (no company-dated catalyst on the calendar; next earnings TBD)
ORCL.bull: OCI/AI backlog story intact and a -16% week sets up a sentiment-reset bounce into the next earnings print.
ORCL.bear: No dated catalyst to stop the bleed, and Sep triple-witching flows can amplify weakness in stretched AI-adjacent names.

NOK.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NOK, and the position just took a -9.9% hit over five days against a flat tape today — that's a name digesting bad news without a clean event to mark the turn. With only triple witching ~80 days out as a calendar item, there's nothing to position into; the job here is to wait for either an earnings date to print or a stabilization in price action before adding to a fresh drawdown.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NOK-specific catalyst dated)
NOK.bull: 5-day washout on a quality networking name with no event risk imminent sets up a mean-revert window if price base-builds from here.
NOK.bear: -9.9% in a week with no dated catalyst means the selling is narrative-driven, and without an earnings print to reset, the bleed can continue.

NBIS.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NBIS, and the only thing ahead is triple witching ~80 days out, which is a tape event, not a fundamental one. Price action is jumpy — up 8.8% today but still -7.8% on the week — which is classic AI-infra volatility on no scheduled news, so action here is driven by flow and headlines rather than a defined window. Without a confirmed print or contract/customer announcement to anchor to, the honest call is to wait for a dated catalyst to surface before leaning either way.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro/flow only; no NBIS-specific event on the calendar)
NBIS.bull: One-day +8.8% snapback shows demand is still there on any AI-infra capacity or customer-win headline.
NBIS.bear: -7.8% on the week with no dated catalyst means the position is hostage to sentiment and any AI-capex wobble.

KO.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for KO — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the feed — so there's no event window to lean into here. The position is small (5 shares) and quietly working, up 3.85% over five sessions on a defensive staple, which is fine but not a setup that demands action. Without a print or dividend date to anchor an add/trim window, this is a sit-and-collect name until the next earnings or ex-div lands on the calendar.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific event scheduled)
KO.bull: Defensive staple grinding higher with the dividend doing the work; nothing here to fight.
KO.bear: No near-term catalyst means no edge — a tape-driven pullback in low-beta staples could erase the 5-day gain quickly.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — there's no dated company catalyst to trade around, so any add/trim window has to be built off price action and lockup mechanics rather than a print. The only thing on the calendar is Sep 18 triple witching, which for a newly-listed name with thin options history can amplify pin/unpin moves but isn't a fundamental catalyst. Until the first earnings date gets confirmed (or a lockup expiry shows up), this is a watch — let the tape define a range before committing to add-on-weakness or trim-into-strength.
SPCX.bull: Scarcity name, post-IPO momentum bid, and first earnings as a public company is a setup catalyst once dated.
SPCX.bear: No confirmed fundamental catalyst, post-IPO lockup overhang risk, and triple witching can whip a thin float.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
